evals.name: game-late-prototype
evals.prompt: What phase is this game in and what should I do next? The project is in ./fixtures/game.
evals.expected_output: Identifies it as a Game (Godot), places it at late prototype / pre-vertical-slice (NOT beta/production/launch) with evidence scorecard, flags the stubbed save system as a downstream blocker, notes the huge scope gap between the GDD (12 levels, 20 enemies, 15 upgrades) and what exists (1 grey-box level, 1 enemy, 0 upgrades), reads git as recently active, proposes a vertical-slice-first roadmap with exit criteria per phase, scores 3 next-slice candidates and recommends ONE vertical-slice slice with /10 score, writes PROJECT_PHASE.md with Next Slice section, and writes IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md with MUST scope and acceptance criteria.

assertions.text: Classifies the project type as Game (and identifies the Godot engine)
Places the current phase at prototype / pre-vertical-slice, NOT beta, production, or launch
Uses evidence scorecard or explicitly contrasts stated vs evidence phase (does not accept README phase claims blindly)
Flags the stubbed save system as a blocker for later phases
Names the scope gap between the GDD's target (12 levels / 20 enemies / 15 upgrades) and what actually exists (1 level, 1 enemy, 0 upgrades)
Reads git history to report momentum (recent, roughly monthly commits, actively worked)
Proposes a next-phase roadmap whose first milestone is a vertical slice / proving one level end-to-end, each phase with an exit criterion, dependencies, and rough effort
Scores 3 next-slice candidates and recommends exactly ONE with a numeric score out of 10
Next slice includes hypothesis, success criteria, and rejected alternatives
Writes a PROJECT_PHASE.md roadmap file into the project with a Next Slice section
Writes IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md with MUST-tagged scope items and acceptance criteria (Engineering Done and Experience Done)
Routes next steps to bundled skills/ paths (not an outer gstack-game install) when suggesting game-review or similar

evals.name: app-mvp-alpha
evals.prompt: I inherited this Flutter project in ./fixtures/app. Where's it at and where should it go?
evals.expected_output: Identifies it as an APP (Flutter), places it at MVP transitioning to alpha with evidence scorecard, notes the real data layer + auth happy path work and persistence exists, flags missing crash reporting/observability and missing error/empty/offline states as gaps before a store launch, notes thin tests (one smoke test), reads git momentum, writes a roadmap with exit criteria toward a store launch, scores and recommends ONE robustness or observability next slice, writes PROJECT_PHASE.md and IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md.

assertions.text: Classifies the project type as APP and identifies Flutter
Places the current phase at MVP / early alpha, NOT beta or launch-ready
Uses evidence scorecard or cites concrete signals for phase placement
Recognizes that the data layer, API auth happy path, and local persistence already work
Flags the absence of crash reporting / analytics / observability as a gap before a public launch
Flags missing robustness states (error / empty / loading / offline)
Notes the thin test coverage (only a smoke test)
Scores 3 next-slice candidates and recommends exactly ONE with a numeric score out of 10
Writes a PROJECT_PHASE.md roadmap with phases, exit criteria, and Next Slice section
Writes IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md with MUST-tagged scope and acceptance criteria

evals.name: web-overclaimed-beta
evals.prompt: Help me plan the roadmap for the website in ./fixtures/web.
evals.expected_output: Identifies it as a WEB project (Next.js marketing site), and critically does NOT take the README's 'basically ready to ship' claim at face value — reads the evidence and places it at alpha/beta, not launch-ready. Flags placeholder content (lorem pricing and about page), the absence of any deploy config (no vercel.json/netlify.toml/CI), missing SEO/meta/sitemap/robots, and the non-responsive nav. Writes a roadmap to close those gaps before launch, scores and recommends ONE launch-gap next slice, writes PROJECT_PHASE.md and IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md.

assertions.text: Classifies the project type as WEB and identifies Next.js
Does NOT accept the README's 'basically ready to ship' claim — explicitly places the project short of launch based on evidence
Flags placeholder / lorem-ipsum content (pricing section and about page) as unfinished
Flags the absence of any deploy configuration or CI/CD (no vercel.json, netlify.toml, or pipeline)
Flags missing SEO essentials (meta/OG tags, sitemap.xml, robots.txt)
Notes the non-responsive nav / lack of responsive work
Scores 3 next-slice candidates and recommends exactly ONE with a numeric score out of 10
Writes a PROJECT_PHASE.md roadmap with phases, exit criteria, and Next Slice section
Writes IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md with MUST-tagged scope (deploy, content, SEO) and acceptance criteria

evals.name: app-routes-to-bundled-engineering
evals.prompt: After planning, what should I do next to build the handoff for this Flutter app? Do not leave the whereto pack.
evals.expected_output: Points at in-pack skills/tdd and/or skills/typeui-fundamentals and/or skills/diagnosing-bugs — never an outer engineering/ or product-planning/prd path. Mentions IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF.md as the build source.

assertions.text: Routes to skills/ paths inside the whereto pack
Does not require an outer product-planning/prd or engineering/ install
Mentions building from IMPLEMENTATION_HANDOFF or equivalent handoff artifact
